candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue figure; clearly labeled as total revenue and matches target definition.
Finance income, not operating revenue; should not be used for REVT.
Finance income, not operating revenue; should not be used for REVT.

candidates.row_label: Income Available to Com Excl ExtraOrd
candidates.why: Represents net income available to common shareholders, not revenue.
Represents comprehensive income, not revenue.
Represents a liability (unearned revenue), not realized revenue.

variables.notes: Total Revenue is the clearly labeled top-line revenue figure; no evidence of multiple revenue lines that need prioritization beyond this.

candidates.why: Most direct production/service-related cost; fits COGS/cost of sales concept best among candidates.
Likely broader operating personnel/overhead costs rather than direct COGS only.
General operating overhead, not strictly cost of goods sold.
Selling expense (SG&A), not COGS.
Appears to be an operating overhead/service cost; classification as COGS is less clear than subcontractor costs.
General occupancy/overhead cost, not direct COGS.
Asset account, not an expense line; not suitable for COGS.
Liability account, not an expense line; not suitable for COGS.

variables.notes: Cost of subcontractors and re-invoiced is the clearest direct cost of providing services; other candidate expenses are broader operating or selling/admin costs.

candidates.why: Represents operating income, not an expense; not SG&A but included as candidate by heuristic.
Classic SG&A-type overhead bucket; fits selling/general/administrative expenses.

variables.notes: There is no single explicit SG&A line; SG&A is spread across several operating expense components. Selected typical SG&A-type components: personnel-related (Personell expenses, Social costs), general overhead (Other operating expenses, Rental Expense, External Services), selling costs (Advertising Expense), and professional/administrative fees (Audit Fees, Tax Advice, Other Fees) plus stock-based compensation. Excluded totals and non-SG&A items (e.g., depreciation, amortization, interest, taxes). Manual review recommended to confirm which of these are considered operating overhead vs possibly classified as COGS by the company. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Personell expenses, [Income Statement] Social costs, [Income Statement] Advertising Expense, [Income Statement] External Services, [Income Statement] Rental Expense, [Income Statement] Audit Fees, [Income Statement] Tax Advice, [Income Statement] Other Fees, [Income Statement] Stock-Based Compensation, Supplemental
No research and development-related line items (e.g., R&D, research costs) are visible in the Income Statement; XRD is treated as zero.

candidates.why: Primary line for interest expense; clear match to target variable.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest component of lease liabilities; should be included if main interest expense line is unavailable for some periods.
Supplemental interest expense line; appears to be an alternative label with potential values in other periods.
Represents profit attributable to minority shareholders, not interest expense.
Same as non-controlling interests; not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; relevant if primary lines are missing for some years.
Equity component, not an income statement interest expense.
Equity component, not an income statement interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: Ordered list provides alternative interest expense labels over time. Both 'Interest expense' rows are included in priority order to capture different periods; lease-related interest and generic 'Finance expense' are fallbacks if the main line is missing. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Finance expense

candidates.why: Component of equity attributable to owners but not the total; better used in combination than alone.
Subtotal combining retained/earned components, but excludes share capital and possibly other components.
Represents total shareholders' equity; with non-controlling interests reported on a separate line, this likely reflects equity attributable to owners of the parent.
Represents total assets/total equity and liabilities; not pure equity.
Technical balancing line; not the substantive equity figure.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Technical balancing line; not the substantive equity figure.

variables.notes: Balance Sheet separately discloses non-controlling interests, so 'Total Equity' is interpreted as equity attributable to owners/parent and is appropriate for BE.

candidates.why: Profit attributable to non-controlling interests; related to minority interest concept but on the income statement.
Duplicate income-statement representation of non-controlling interests.
Balance sheet line for equity attributable to non-controlling interests; ideal for MIB in BE+MIB denominator.

candidates.row_label: CI Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income attributable to minority interests; not the balance sheet stock of minority equity.

variables.notes: Mapped to balance sheet non-controlling interest lines to capture the equity amount (MIB). Both spellings are included to cover different periods; later processing will take the first available value per year. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Balance Sheet] Miniority Interest
